text.title: Win-Loss Interview Program Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Win-Loss Interview Program application MUST enforce, plus a skip-this-methodology fallback. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Interview ratio MUST be 50% won deals + 50% lost deals. Lost-only is pessimism bias; won-only is sales-narrative confirmation.
rule.rationale: Balanced samples produce materially better insights than either-only; both halves answer different questions (why we win / why we lose) that compose into roadmap signal.

rule.statement: Interviewer MUST NOT be the AE/CSM who worked the deal. Use a PM, researcher, or peer rep from a different territory.
rule.rationale: Interviewees give socially-desirable answers to the person they negotiated with; separating interviewer from seller is the standard bias control in win/loss practice.

rule.statement: Script MUST include open + evidence-anchored prompts: 'What alternatives did you consider?', 'What would have made you go the other way?', 'What surprised you?'. Leading questions ('Was our price the issue?') are rejected.
rule.rationale: Leading questions corrupt the signal; open + anchored questions retrieve actual purchase reasoning rather than reasoning the interviewee thinks the company wants to hear.

rule.statement: Interview MUST happen within 30 days of deal close. After 30 days, memory degrades and rationalisation grows.
rule.rationale: Empirical recall research is consistent: post-30-day interviews retrieve mostly rationalisation; the methodology preserves the recency window.

rule.statement: Quarterly synthesis MUST be distributed to PM (product/roadmap), marketing (messaging/positioning), and sales (objection-handling). Single-channel synthesis is rejected.
rule.rationale: Insights die in inboxes; three-channel distribution is the only configuration that consistently moves win-rates because each function takes a different action from the same finding.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition / triggered skip, and route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Methodology hygiene: mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
